title: Source registry + per-source license-verification protocol
context: Most authoritative patient-nutrition content is copyrighted (ACS, ESPEN, ASPEN, ASCO, Academy, WCRF, Macmillan, Cancer Research UK, and much of NICE). The project's central licensing rule is synthesize-and-cite, never republish copyrighted text. This M0 task establishes the registry of authoritative sources with verified reuse terms and a protocol so that no source informs shipped content until its license is verified and recorded. WHO's CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO is specifically flagged as incompatible with CC-BY reproduction. This task is riskTier:high because an unverified license informs copyright exposure and legal liability.
objective: Establish the source registry schema and document, with verified reuse terms, for the initial set of authoritative bodies. Define the reuse-mode taxonomy (reproduce-allowed vs cite-and-synthesize-only) and the protocol requiring every source to be verified and recorded before its content informs anything that ships. Implement the registry as a structured data file that the pipeline license-check gate can consume.
acceptanceCriteria: Registry schema defined per source: name, jurisdiction, URL, verified reuse terms (quoted from the source's license page), reuse-mode (reproduce-allowed | cite-and-synthesize-only), retrieval date, legal-status note

Reuse terms verified and recorded for the initial authoritative bodies: NCI/PDQ (US Govt public domain → reproduce-allowed with attribution), NHS/nhs.uk (Open Government Licence v3.0 → reproduce-allowed with OGL attribution), WHO (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO → cite-and-synthesize-only; incompatible with CC-BY reproduction), ACS, ESPEN, ASPEN, ASCO/Cancer.Net, Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics, WCRF/AICR, Macmillan, Cancer Research UK, NICE (all copyrighted → cite-and-synthesize-only)

Central rule stated and actionable: synthesize original plain-language text and cite; reproduce wording only from reproduce-allowed sources with required attribution; no source informs shipped content until verified and recorded in the registry

Protocol document specifies the process for adding new sources: research the license page, quote the reuse terms, assign reuse-mode, record retrieval date, and have the entry reviewed before the source is enabled
Reviewed and approved by the project maintainer and an oncology RDN/CSO (for clinical-source coverage); oncology-dietitian review recorded
output: A source registry file (e.g., sources/registry.yaml or registry.json) with verified license entries for the initial authoritative bodies, plus a license-verification protocol document defining the process for adding new sources and the synthesize-and-cite rule that all pipeline checks enforce.
